events.content: "Evidence": "npx markdownlint-cli2 executed on touched .md files; 0 errors reported"
"action": "Wired orphan-ref-validator as fourth Mandatory Exit Gate in .claude/commands/build.md with VERDICT: CRITICAL_FAIL blocking semantics"

"action": "Pre-push surfaced two blockers: marketplace count divergence with canonical validator_marketplace_counts.py (my naive iterdir counted templates/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md), and security-suppression comment. Aligned enumerate_count to canonical strategies per canonical-source-mirror.md (excluded AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md from agents; recursive walk for commands). Replaced try/except ImportError with __package__ check to remove type: ignore. Ran build/scripts/build_all.py to regenerate src/copilot-cli/skills/orphan-ref-validator/ and src/copilot-cli/skills/build/SKILL.md mirrors."

"action": "PR review feedback round 1: 19 inline comments. Critical fixes - (1) Success envelope contract per ADR-056: Success now reflects scan execution not finding presence, true on CRITICAL_FAIL with verdict expressing it; (2) _resolve_repo_root raises RepoRootError on missing/non-dir paths, main maps to exit 2; (3) enumerate_skills returns None when dir absent (was returning empty set, masked downstream warn-vs-critical disambiguation); (4) COUNT_CLAIM_RE rewritten to mirror canonical COUNT_PATTERN byte-for-byte (handles 'reusable skills', 'slash commands', 'lifecycle hooks', 'specialized agent definitions'); (5) enumerate_count switches on canonical labels with legacy aliases; (6) _walk_targets uses iterdir+prune instead of rglob+postfilter for performance; (7) walked-file containment recheck post symlink resolution (CWE-22 hardening); (8) count_claim emission delegated to canonical validate_marketplace_counts.py per canonical-source-mirror.md (multi-plugin manifests need YAML config); enforce_counts param reserved for PR2; (9) TASK-007 frontmatter merged into one block (assignee was outside metadata); (10) DESIGN-008 added required sections (Requirements Addressed, Design Overview, Component Details, Technology Decisions, Security Considerations, Testing Strategy); (11) REQ-008/DESIGN-008/TASK-008 deferred-list updated to reflect AC3 ships in PR1 and AC4 emission delegated; (12) SKILL.md flag table reflects actual defaults (no .claude/skills/ in default scope); (13) build.md gate 4 uses Skill() invocation instead of hardcoded path. 40 tests pass; self-test PASS."

"action": "PR review feedback round 2: per-call enumerate_count cache to eliminate redundant filesystem walks per gemini perf comment; REQ-008 and DESIGN-008 related fields complete forward-trace (REQ -> DESIGN -> TASK); TASK-008 status changed from draft to in-progress and complexity: M added to satisfy spec validator; episode-2026-05-10-session-1830.json hand-rewritten with valid event-type enums (milestone/error/recovery), explicit decisions, and lessons learned; test_scan.py uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location with file-keyed module names to avoid sys.modules collision between .claude/skills and src/copilot-cli/skills mirrored test suites."

"action": "Round 4 (5-axis /review verdicts: REQUEST_CHANGES from architect/security/qa + taste-lints FAIL on scan.py 719 lines). Critical fixes: (1) Extract walking.py (CWE-22/CWE-59 hardening: is_safe_subdirectory rejects symlink directories outside repo before recursion); align EXCLUDE_DIR_NAMES with canonical _EXCLUDED_DIRS (added worktrees, cache; kept references, templates as documented divergence). (2) Extract counts.py to mirror canonical-source verbatim and centralize the working-tree enumeration. (3) Extract envelope.py with Finding/ScanResult and render_envelope/render_error_envelope; expose render_error_envelope for the exit-2 path so configuration errors emit ADR-056 Success=false envelope (was unreachable). (4) Extend secret denylist (id_rsa, id_ed25519, id_ecdsa, id_dsa, .pfx, .p12, .netrc, .npmrc, .pypirc, credentials). (5) enumerate_skills returns None when .claude/skills/ is a regular file (vendored-install corruption); was raising NotADirectoryError. (6) Decompose _iter_dir_pruned into _iter_entry + _within_size_cap to satisfy cyclomatic complexity ≤10. (7) Document ignore-directive bypass as intentional with code-review compensating control in DESIGN-008. New tests for symlink-to-directory containment, .claude/skills as a file, CWD-not-in-git fallback, glob target expansion, and exit-2 envelope shape (47 cases). scan.py 719 -> 455 lines; all 5 modules clean under taste-lints (0 errors)."

"action": "Round 5 (12 review threads + 2 CI fails after round-4 push). Critical fixes: (1) STEP-0-METRICS.md was accidentally committed to .agents/sessions/ in round-4 git add -A; the path triggered Session Protocol Validation which expected JSON via Convert-SessionToJson.ps1 (script absent). Moved file to .agents/metrics/STEP-0-METRICS.md to remove from session validator scope. (2) --include-adrs and --include-skill-descriptions silently ignored when --targets supplied; now log a WARNING explaining the precedence. (3) reset_count_cache() now called at scan() entry; programmatic re-scans no longer see stale counts (devin's observation about latent PR2 bug). Doc accuracy fixes: SKILL.md verification checklist test count 34 -> 47 (and now reads 'currently 47 cases; count grows'); EXCLUDE_DIR_NAMES list now lists worktrees/cache; Reference detection table corrected (skill_name regex requires hyphen; count_claim shows canonical COUNT_PATTERN with delegation note); Path safety section lists the full secret denylist; Failure modes table replaced count_claim WARN row with delegation explanation and split symlink-loop row into directory/file. TASK-008 milestone count 42 -> 47. Serena memory aligned with shipped state (AC3 implemented; canonical 23/23/35/68 counts)."

"action": "Round 6 (/test 6-gate run: NFR analyst WARN with 1 critical + DX critic NEEDS_REVISION with 5 critical). NFR fixes: (1) scan_file refactored from 127-line method into a thin orchestrator delegating to _check_skill_refs / _check_script_refs / _check_count_claims plus _count_warn_finding / _count_critical_finding helpers. (2) _WHITESPACE_RE compiled module-scope (was inline re.sub per match). (3) counts.py walks now apply _EXCLUDED_DIR_NAMES future-proofing (defense in depth if a vendor dir ever lands in .claude). (4) MAX_FINDINGS=500 cap with synthetic warn finding bounds memory on pathological repos. (5) Patterns extracted to patterns.py (regex + extractors + line ignore directive helper); scan.py 560 -> 478 lines. DX fixes: (1) --include-* warning emitted to stderr regardless of log level. (2) build.md gate 4 description includes the diagnostic invocation literal. (3) SKILL.md adds dedicated 'Ignore directives' subsection with placement rules and the 50-line window. (4) New 'When the /build gate fails' section with three-step recovery + 'Investigation workflow' for opt-in flags. (5) Fixed pytest invocation inconsistency (uv run pytest everywhere). 48 tests pass; scan PASS; all 6 modules clean under taste-lints (0 errors)."

"action": "Round 7 (24 review threads). Critical fixes: (1) Error.Code now integer 2 + Type 'InvalidParams' per skill-output.schema.json (was string 'ConfigurationError', schema violation). (2) File-symlink containment: walking.py _is_safe_file resolves and rejects file symlinks whose target escapes repo_root (was: only directories checked). Test added. (3) In-repo symlink cycle guard: _iter_dir_pruned tracks visited canonical paths via Path.resolve() and short-circuits on revisit. Test added. (4) walk_targets applies SCAN_FILE_SUFFIXES to direct file targets (was: only enforced during directory walk). Test added. (5) REQ-008 category 'maintainability' -> 'non-functional' (valid spec enum). (6) VERDICT: ERROR documented in REQ-008 AC5, SKILL.md, Serena memory (was: contract said only PASS|WARN|CRITICAL_FAIL but ERROR shipped). (7) build.md gate 4 documents repo-wide scope rationale and points at directives for opt-out. (8) Mirror-divergence fixes (Extension Points pointing at scan.py for *_REF_RE -> patterns.py; build.md diagnostic command no longer hardcodes path). (9) Canonical-mirror docstrings rewritten: walking.py now quotes _EXCLUDED_DIRS byte-for-byte, counts.py quotes the project-toolkit YAML stanza byte-for-byte, both list divergences honestly. (10) Test count drift: removed pinned numbers from SKILL.md / TASK-008 / DESIGN-008 / Serena memory. 51 tests pass; scan PASS; 6 modules clean."

"action": "Round 8 (6 review threads). Critical fixes: (1) walk_targets now rejects out-of-repo direct targets at entry (defense in depth; scan() also checks). (2) Cross-platform module key in test_scan.py uses Path.parts intersection on '.claude' instead of substring 'claude/skills' that fails on Windows. (3) skill-count divergence from canonical _count_skill_dirs documented in counts.py (intentional: orphan-ref-validator counts only valid skills with SKILL.md; canonical counts plain subdirs because it reports declared marketplace inventory). (4) SKILL.md References section drops internal tracker IDs for downstream-mirror cleanliness. (5) New 'Path conventions' section documents that absolute paths assume canonical Claude install layout and Copilot CLI mirror users adapt to their install root; Skill() invocation form remains platform-agnostic. 51 tests pass; scan PASS."

"action": "Round 9 (8 review threads). Critical fix: max_findings cap was off by one (truncation finding appended after extending findings list); now reserves a slot before extending so the total never exceeds the budget. Test asserts the hard bound. Doc fixes: (1) SKILL.md Inputs CLI usage now lists --include-adrs and --include-skill-descriptions. (2) TASK-008-02 reference patterns in fenced code block (was inline backticks-in-backticks that broke markdown rendering). (3) TASK-008-03 rewritten to reflect AC4 wedge: extraction only, emission delegated to canonical, --enforce-counts reserved for PR2. (4) Session log step 16 dropped pinned numbers (9 commits / 32 files) that contradicted auto-extracted episode metrics. (5) SKILL.md Path conventions and References cleaned for mirror; mirror tracker IDs already addressed in round 7. 51 tests pass; scan PASS."

"action": "Round 10 (3 review threads, all minor). (1) Removed unused VERSION import from scan.py (envelope module already uses it internally). (2) SKILL.md pre-push hook wording corrected to describe push-changeset scoping (git diff --name-only @{push}..HEAD) instead of staged-diff (which is wrong context for pre-push). (3) TASK-008 Definition of Done updated to reflect PR1-only completion criteria: AC8 and --enforce-counts are PR2; /test gate is PR2; the previous DoD claimed all 9 ACs and full /test wiring which contradicted the PR2-deferred sections of the same task file. 51 tests pass; scan PASS."

"action": "Round 11 (11 review threads). Code: (1) Truncation finding now uses kind=scan_truncated instead of parse_error (added scan_truncated to Kind Literal) so downstream consumers do not conflate memory-bound halt with file parse failure. Test updated. (2) reset_count_cache() docstring now explicitly documents the limitation that direct callers of enumerate_count/enumerate_skills must clear the cache themselves if they mutate the filesystem between calls (cursor's flag for the latent footgun). Specs: (3) DESIGN-008 component table relocates extractors to patterns.py (was scan.py from before round-6 extraction). (4) DESIGN-008 language requirement Python >=3.10 (matches pyproject.toml; was unnecessarily Python 3.14+). (5) DESIGN-008 /test Gate 5 wiring section relabeled 'deferred to PR2' to match REQ-008/TASK-008 (was inviting readers to assume PR1 ships it). (6) DESIGN-008 Failure Recovery table corrected: unreadable files no longer claim a parse_error finding (skipped silently); malformed JSON no longer claims a count_claim finding (delegated); added rows for scan_truncated, symlink-out, symlink-cycle. Doc drift: (7-10) Removed pinned '34 tests pass' / '34 pytest cases' from session log evidence + step 2 + step 5, episode events, Serena memory."

lessons: "action": "PR review feedback round 2: per-call enumerate_count cache to eliminate redundant filesystem walks per gemini perf comment; REQ-008 and DESIGN-008 related fields complete forward-trace (REQ -> DESIGN -> TASK); TASK-008 status changed from draft to in-progress and complexity: M added to satisfy spec validator; episode-2026-05-10-session-1830.json hand-rewritten with valid event-type enums (milestone/error/recovery), explicit decisions, and lessons learned; test_scan.py uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location with file-keyed module names to avoid sys.modules collision between .claude/skills and src/copilot-cli/skills mirrored test suites."
